0	//! Trivia such as comments and irregular whitespaces
1	//!
2	//! This module provides utilities for working with source code comments,
3	//! including efficient range-based queries and iteration over comment collections.
4	
5	use std::{
6	    cmp::Ordering,
7	    iter::FusedIterator,
8	    ops::{Bound, RangeBounds},
9	};
10	
11	use oxc_span::Span;
12	
13	use crate::ast::comment::*;
14	
15	/// Create an iterator over comments within a given range
16	///
17	/// Returns a double-ended iterator that yields comments whose starting positions
18	/// fall within the specified range bounds.
19	///
20	/// # Arguments
21	///
22	/// * `comments` - A slice of comments sorted by starting position
23	/// * `range` - The range of positions to filter comments by
24	///
25	/// # Examples
26	///
27	/// ```ignore
28	/// let comments_in_range = comments_range(&all_comments, 10..50);
29	/// for comment in comments_in_range {
30	///     // Process comments starting between positions 10 and 50
31	/// }
32	/// ```
33	pub fn comments_range<R>(comments: &[Comment], range: R) -> CommentsRange<'_>
34	where
35	    R: RangeBounds<u32>,
36	{
37	    CommentsRange::new(comments, range.start_bound().cloned(), range.end_bound().cloned())
38	}
39	
40	/// Check if there are any comments within a given span
41	///
42	/// Returns `true` if at least one comment starts within the specified span.
43	///
44	/// # Arguments
45	///
46	/// * `comments` - A slice of comments sorted by starting position
47	/// * `span` - The span to check for comments
48	///
49	/// # Examples
50	///
51	/// ```ignore
52	/// if has_comments_between(&comments, node.span) {
53	///     // Handle nodes that have comments within their span
54	/// }
55	/// ```
56	pub fn has_comments_between(comments: &[Comment], span: Span) -> bool {
57	    comments_range(comments, span.start..span.end).count() > 0
58	}
59	
60	/// Check if a position falls within any comment
61	///
62	/// Returns `true` if the specified position is inside any comment's span.
63	/// The start position is included, but the end position is excluded.
64	///
65	/// Uses binary search for efficient lookup in O(log n) time.
66	///
67	/// # Arguments
68	///
69	/// * `comments` - A slice of comments sorted by starting position
70	/// * `pos` - The position to check
71	///
72	/// # Examples
73	///
74	/// ```ignore
75	/// // Comment spans from position 10 to 20
76	/// assert!(is_inside_comment(&comments, 15));  // Inside comment
77	/// assert!(!is_inside_comment(&comments, 25)); // Outside comment
78	/// ```
79	pub fn is_inside_comment(comments: &[Comment], pos: u32) -> bool {
80	    get_comment_at(comments, pos).is_some()
81	}
82	
83	/// Get the comment containing a position, if any
84	///
85	/// Returns a reference to the comment if the specified position is inside any
86	/// comment's span. The start position is included, but the end position is excluded.
87	///
88	/// Uses binary search for efficient lookup in O(log n) time.
89	///
90	/// # Arguments
91	///
92	/// * `comments` - A slice of comments sorted by starting position
93	/// * `pos` - The position to check
94	///
95	/// # Examples
96	///
97	/// ```ignore
98	/// // Comment spans from position 10 to 20
99	/// if let Some(comment) = get_comment_at(&comments, 15) {
100	///     // Position is inside this comment
101	///     println!("Comment spans {:?}", comment.span);
102	/// }
103	/// ```
104	pub fn get_comment_at(comments: &[Comment], pos: u32) -> Option<&Comment> {
105	    comments
106	        .binary_search_by(|c| {
107	            if pos < c.span.start {
108	                Ordering::Greater
109	            } else if pos >= c.span.end {
110	                Ordering::Less
111	            } else {
112	                Ordering::Equal
113	            }
114	        })
115	        .ok()
116	        .map(|idx| &comments[idx])
117	}
118	
119	/// Double-ended iterator over a range of comments, by starting position

input.description: Show formatter diff and working tree status

content.text:
Implemented the formatter fix for empty TypeScript enums with dangling single-line comments.

Changed:
- `crates/oxc_formatter/src/print/mod.rs`
  - Empty enum bodies now format dangling comments using `with_block_indent()` instead of a grouped dangling comment plus `soft_line_break()`.
  - This preserves the newline before the closing `}` so the brace is not swallowed into the `//` comment.

Validation:
- Added a temporary fixture reproducing the issue.
- Confirmed the formatter output matches the expected Prettier-style layout.
- Removed the temporary fixture and snapshot afterward, per instructions.
- Re-ran the removed fixture filter to confirm no temporary test remains:
  - `cargo test -p oxc_formatter --test mod ts__comments__empty_enum_line_comment -- --nocapture`

Notes:
- `just fmt` could not be run because `just` is not installed in this environment.
- `cargo fmt -p oxc_formatter` also could not be run because `rustfmt` is not installed for the active toolchain.
- No commits were made.

result:
Implemented the formatter fix for empty TypeScript enums with dangling single-line comments.

Changed:
- `crates/oxc_formatter/src/print/mod.rs`
  - Empty enum bodies now format dangling comments using `with_block_indent()` instead of a grouped dangling comment plus `soft_line_break()`.
  - This preserves the newline before the closing `}` so the brace is not swallowed into the `//` comment.

Validation:
- Added a temporary fixture reproducing the issue.
- Confirmed the formatter output matches the expected Prettier-style layout.
- Removed the temporary fixture and snapshot afterward, per instructions.
- Re-ran the removed fixture filter to confirm no temporary test remains:
  - `cargo test -p oxc_formatter --test mod ts__comments__empty_enum_line_comment -- --nocapture`

Notes:
- `just fmt` could not be run because `just` is not installed in this environment.
- `cargo fmt -p oxc_formatter` also could not be run because `rustfmt` is not installed for the active toolchain.
- No commits were made.
